data:
P  R  E  O  N  E    E  N  T  E  R  P  R  I  S  E
PreOne Enterprise
Backend Technical Design
Backend Architecture — Enterprise Preschool Operating System
Document Version: 1.0
Status: Backend Architecture Freeze
Date: 2026-07-14
Product: PreOne — Enterprise Preschool Operating System
Predecessor: Vision v1.0, BRC v1.0, Master PRD v1.0, DDD v1.0, ADR Catalog, ERD v3.0, Prisma Schema v3.0, API Contract Catalog
Successor: Backend Implementation, Unit Tests, Integration Tests, Deployment, Production Release
Classification: Internal Engineering Reference
Prepared by: PreOne Architecture & Engineering Team

PreOne Platform                                        Backend TD v1.0
Strategic intent — BTD च्या non-functional requirements (scale, compliance) Vision शी align
176 rules — BTD च्या Domain Events + Specifications BRC चे implementation
Functional requirements — BTD चे API endpoints PRD च्या FRs चे direct mapping
Domain model — BTD च्या NestJS modules DDD च्या bounded contexts चे implementation
Decisions — BTD प्रत्येक architectural choice ला ADR reference करतो
Physical schema — BTD च्या Prisma models ERD चे direct translation
Code-first schema — BTD च्या repositories चे data access layer
Endpoint contracts — BTD च्या controllers चे spec
Modular DI, decorators, microservices, WebSocket gateway, scheduling, OpenAPI via @nestjs/swagger
Production-grade Angular-style DI; ecosystem mature; squad familiarity
Strict mode, path aliases, decorators, const assertions, satisfies operator
Type safety at compile time; DDD-friendly with branded types & value objects
Schema-first, type-safe client, migrations, multi-schema, read-replica routing (client extensions)
Type safety end-to-end; migration history; excellent Postgres support
RLS, partial indexes, JSONB, generated columns, pgcrypto, pgvector (for AI), logical replication
ACID compliance; RLS for tenant isolation; pgvector for embeddings
Permission cache, menu cache, BullMQ backend, rate-limit store, pub/sub
Sub-ms reads; supports BullMQ persistence; Redis Streams for events
Repeatable jobs, delayed jobs, priorities, rate-limited queues, DLQ, flow producer
Battle-tested on Redis; observability via Bull Board; sandboxed processors
Access tokens (15 min) + refresh tokens (30 d, rotated), RS256 signed
Adaptive transport, rooms per tenant+branch, sticky-session via Redis adapter
Bi-directional push for chat, attendance live, dashboards
Pre-signed uploads, server-side encryption (SSE-KMS), lifecycle to Glacier, versioning
Durable file storage; lifecycle to Glacier for archives; KMS encryption
DTO validation pipeline; schema composition; custom decorators
Pipe integration; fail-fast on invalid input
JSON structured logs, child loggers, redact PII, async transport
Low overhead; structured for Loki/ELK; correlation by traceId
Auto-instrumentation for HTTP/PG/Redis, custom spans, OTLP exporter
Native ESM, snapshot, in-source testing, coverage via v8
Fast startup; Jest-compatible API; TS native
Real Postgres + Redis containers per test run
Real DB semantics; no mocked Prisma drift
Lint, format, type-check on staged files
Catch issues before push; block broken commits
Auto-generated module diagrams from NestJS sources
Standard OWASP headers; payload size reduction
Application bootstrap, global modules, filters, pipes, interceptors, guards
Crown of the process; one-time wiring
Shared kernel — base classes, error codes, result types, decorators, utilities, types
Typed config loaders (env, secrets, tenant config, feature flags)
Cross-module infra — Prisma client, Redis client, S3 client, EventBus, Mailer, BullMQ registrars
Concrete adapters; domain sees only interfaces
Shared domain primitives — UoW base, base entity, base aggregate, value object base, specification base
DDD kernel reused by all modules
Business modules (one folder per bounded context)
HTTP controllers — versioned, route mapped, Swagger tagged
Controllers MUST NOT contain business logic; only DTO validation + dispatch
CQRS query objects for read side
Command & query handlers — orchestrate aggregate + UoW + events
Single handler per command/query; Idempotent via idempotency-key
Zod schemas + class-validator decorators + Swagger metadata
Three-way mapping; never leak Prisma types to controllers
Pure TS; zero infra deps; enforce invariants
Immutable value objects (Address, Money, Age, TimeWindow)
Equals by value; throw on invalid construction
Implemented in infrastructure/; mockable in tests
Domain services — pure business rules involving multiple aggregates
Specification pattern for complex predicate logic
TTL + invalidation versioned by cache key prefix
Event bus adapters — in-memory (sync) + Redis Streams (async)
BullMQ job producers + processors for this module
Presenters — format domain responses for HTTP/WebSocket views
NestJS module wiring — providers, controllers, imports, exports
Controllers call application services / command handlers
Controllers must never touch aggregates/entities directly
Handlers orchestrate aggregates via repository ports
Repositories injected as abstractions; never concrete class
Domain knows nothing about use cases
Domain is pure TypeScript; zero NestJS/Prisma imports
Cross-module imports forbidden — use integration events
Infra must not know about handlers
Modules talk via ACL (Anti-Corruption Layer) or events
Global guard; opt-out via @Public() for login/health
Custom decorator backed by Casbin policies
Fail fast on schema violation; 422 response
Key from header or body hash; 24h TTL in Redis
All required + optional fields, validation decorators, Swagger metadata
Sanitised output; never includes PII like Aadhaar
Lightweight fields for list view; pagination metadata
Filterable fields, sort spec, pagination params
Full-text search query, fuzzy match, suggestions
Array of CreateStudentDto + idempotency key per item
Coordinate multiple aggregates in a single transaction
Single aggregate = single transaction; multi-aggregate via saga
Outbox pattern — events written to DB, then published async
Call ACL of another bounded context
Finance → Identity ACL for user lookup
Idempotent reads only; cache key = method signature + args
Return cached response if key exists; else compute + cache
Audit log entry per command, immutable
Encapsulate logic that doesn't fit a single aggregate
RefundPolicy decides refund amount given cancellation date
No two students same roll number in same section
Single aggregate by id; returns null if not found or soft-deleted
Throws NotFoundException if missing — fail-fast path
For pagination totals; cheaper than full fetch
Insert or update (decides by presence of id); writes within UoW
Soft-delete (sets deletedAt); hard delete only via admin tooling
Pessimistic lock for concurrent writes (SELECT FOR UPDATE)
Optimistic locking — fails on version mismatch
Returns unit-of-work handle for explicit control
Create new student record + initial profile
Enroll student in section for academic year
Publish term report card to parents
Issue goods from store to department
Student id, name, branch, section, createdBy
Sync: parent welcome email; Async: CRM lead conversion
Sync: cache invalidation; Async: audit log
From class, to class, academic year
Async: new fee plan, new section assignment
Async: archival at old branch, new record at new branch
Application id, student name, parent contact
Sync: confirmation SMS; Async: counsellor assignment
Async: rejection email, CRM status update
Sync: parent SMS; Async: monthly summary
Invoice id, student, amount, due date
Sync: receipt SMS; Async: ledger entry, GST return prep
Sync: credentials setup; Async: asset assignment
Async: asset recovery, access revoke, final settlement
Sync: manager notification; Async: calendar block
Staff id, leave type, dates, approver
Async: PR auto-creation if rule exists
Sync: WebSocket push; Async: push for offline users
Async: integrity check, alert on failure
Flag key, old value, new value, changedBy
Sync: cache bust; Async: audit log
Notify student record of pending invoice
Sync (HTTP) — admissions waits for student id
Burst during attendance mark, fee due, report publish
Bulk admissions email uses separate child queue
WhatsApp rate limits are stricter than SMS
Token-bucket per tenant to control cost
Audit log archival to S3 Glacier
Async upload to S3 + email link
Slack webhook after 3 consecutive failures in same queue
Auto-page on-call engineer for critical queues (notification, sms, backup)
BullMQ sandbox mode for report/ai queues
Invalidated on role change, permission grant/revoke
Avoids DB hit on every page load
Auto-expire; manual bust via admin tool
Sliding window via Redis sorted set
Strong consistency; slightly higher write latency
App → cache → ack; async DB write
High write throughput; risk of data loss on crash
App checks cache; on miss loads from DB
Prevents cache stampede on hot keys
Orchestration via state machine + compensating actions
Each step = own transaction; rollback via compensation
DB transaction writes events to outbox table; publisher drains to stream
WHERE version = expected; throw on 0 rows affected
Hold lock for shortest possible duration
No lock acquired; replica routing possible
Schema shape, required fields, type coercion
Business invariants (e.g., valid phone, age range)
Cross-entity rules (e.g., unique roll number in section)
Last line of defense — should never trigger if above layers work
Client error — retry with corrected input
Client must change request — retry with different data
Client may retry with fresh fetch
Correlates to logs — quote in support tickets
Field-level errors (for ValidationException only) — { field, code, message }
Request path that triggered the error
Access token 15 min (RS256 signed); refresh token 30 d (rotated, HttpOnly cookie)
Each refresh issues new token; old one blacklisted
Detects token theft — both tokens used = revoke all
Every query scoped by tenantId + branchId from JWT
Tenant isolation at ORM layer (Prisma middleware)
Defense in depth — even buggy query returns only tenant data
Branch ID in JWT; query filter enforced
Academic year ID in context; filter on every academic query
Default 100 req/min; stricter on auth routes (5/min)
DOMPurify on rich text; parameterised queries (Prisma)
PII fields encrypted at rest (pgcrypto); masked in logs
Every state-changing action logged with user + diff
No secrets in env files or code
Snyk + npm audit in CI; fail on high+ vulns
OWASP ZAP scan on staging weekly
Populated on login; verified on every request
Last line of defense; failsafe if Prisma bypassed
Cached in Redis; hot reload on change
New tenant = new schema? No — shared schema with discriminator
Only platform team has this — audited
Soft delete + 90-day retention + then hard purge
DPDP Act right to erasure compliance
branchId from JWT or query param
Stack trace + code (only on error level)
Operation duration in ms (for performance logs)
Very detailed — SQL queries, cache hits/misses
Enabled on staging; per-route enable on prod via feature flag
Normal operation — request received, command executed, event published
Unexpected but recoverable — rate limit hit, retry scheduled, fallback used
Failures requiring attention — external service down, DB error, unhandled exception
Always enabled; alert on >0 in critical paths
Process must exit — config invalid, port in use
RED (Rate, Errors, Duration) per endpoint; Saturation (DB pool, Redis memory); Utilisation (CPU, RAM, disk)
Structured; traceId correlation; 30-day hot retention + 1-year cold (S3)
Distributed traces across services; span per DB/Redis/HTTP call
Error rate, latency p95, saturation thresholds
Per-module dashboard + system overview + tenant health
Login, mark attendance, generate invoice every 5 min
Process can serve traffic (DB, Redis connected)
Pure TS — aggregate, entity, VO, spec, policy, domain service
Handler logic with mocked repositories + event bus
Real DB — verify Prisma mapping + migrations
HTTP layer — DTO validation, auth guards, response shape
End-to-end within module — controller → handler → repo → DB
Failure injection — DB kill, Redis flush, network partition
Prisma client extension routes reads to replica
Bulk insert/update via Prisma createMany / updateMany
Cursor-based for large tables; offset for small
EXPLAIN ANALYZE in CI on slow queries
Prisma includes / DataLoader for nested reads
Lint rule: no nested find in loop
Prisma select() to fetch only needed columns
Pre-signed URLs still valid through CDN
All PRs merged to main; no unmerged feature branches
Prisma migrations applied to staging; verified
All tests green in CI; coverage ≥ 80%
All required env vars set in production secrets store
New features behind flags; default off
Documented rollback steps; tested in staging
Dashboards + alerts configured for new metrics
New log fields indexed in Loki
k6 load test on critical new endpoints; meets SLO
Snyk + SonarCloud clean; no new vulnerabilities
README + API docs updated; Swagger regenerated
Operational runbook for new module updated
On-call rotation updated; new alerts mapped to team
BullMQ uses DB 0; cache uses DB 1
For static assets + pre-signed S3 URLs
Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — properties of reliable database transactions
Anti-Corruption Layer — translation layer between bounded contexts to prevent model pollution
Architecture Decision Record — short document capturing a single architectural decision
Cluster of domain objects treated as a single unit for data consistency; has one root
Explicit boundary within which a domain model makes sense and terms have one meaning
Redis-backed queue library for Node.js; used for background jobs in PreOne
Authorization library supporting RBAC, ABAC, ACL; used for PreOne permission checks
Command Query Responsibility Segregation — separate models for reads vs writes
Dead Letter Queue — queue for messages that failed processing after retries
Data Transfer Object — serialisable object for crossing process/network boundaries
Domain-Driven Design — software approach that mirrors business domain in code
Horizontal Pod Autoscaler — Kubernetes controller that scales pods based on metrics
Property where repeated operations produce same result — critical for safe retries
JSON Web Token — compact, signed token for stateless authentication
OpenTelemetry Protocol — wire format for exporting traces, metrics, logs
Writing events to DB table in same transaction as data; async publisher drains table
Personally Identifiable Information — data that can identify an individual
Type-safe ORM for Node.js / TypeScript — schema-first, generates client
Reduction in Force — but in PreOne context: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for AI features
Role-Based Access Control — permissions assigned to roles; users inherit via role
In-memory key-value store — used for cache, pub/sub, queues, rate limiting
Row-Level Security — PostgreSQL feature that filters rows by user/tenant context
Pattern for managing multi-step transactions with compensating actions on failure
Service Level Objective — target metric for service quality (e.g., 99.9% uptime)
Pattern encapsulating business rule as composable predicate (e.g., AgeEligibilitySpec)
Customer organisation in multi-tenant SaaS — in PreOne, a school
Unit of Work — pattern that tracks changes and commits them as a single transaction
Immutable object identified by its values (e.g., Address, Money) — no identity
Modern test runner for Vite-powered projects — used for PreOne unit tests
Full-duplex communication protocol over TCP — used for real-time features in PreOne
Physical schema — repository data access layer
